items.title: 🔒 Security hardening
items.body: All AI-generated tracker text (names, thoughts, scene fields, emoji, imported presets) is now escaped before it touches the page — malicious markup in a character name or a shared tracker preset can no longer run script in your browser. Update recommended for everyone.

items.title: Doom Counter progress survives chat switches
items.body: Doom Counter state, the per-chat Knives toggle, and freshly generated tracker data now save immediately instead of on a delay that a quick chat switch could silently drop. Fast switching also no longer flashes the previous chat's scene tracker into the new one.

items.title: Scene Tracker toggles agree with each other
items.body: The field toggles in the settings panel and the Tracker Editor now stay in sync both ways, and Weather — previously missing from the Tracker Editor — has its own row there. Failed saves in the Character Workshop now show an error toast instead of silently losing your edits.

items.title: Snappier rendering
items.body: Tracker data is parsed once per update instead of 6-8 times per message, portrait card back-faces stopped re-reading everything per character, and dragging the Scene Tracker color/size sliders no longer rebuilds every header per frame.

items.title: English-only from here
items.body: The Russian and Traditional Chinese locale files (and the language selector) are gone — almost none of the current UI was ever translated, so they only pretended to work. If you'd like to maintain a translation, open an issue on GitHub.
